title: fix: override existing app proxy domains
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Passes override_existing_origin when attaching app public domains to pollinations-proxy.
- Handles cutovers where the public hostname is still attached to an older Worker or Pages custom domain.
- Unblocks the Websim public-domain cutover.

Checks:
- npx biome check --write apps/_scripts/deploy-app.js
- git diff --check

gist.headline: Smooth Domain Cutovers
gist.blurb: Fixed a deployment hiccup so app domains can smoothly override existing origins. No more traffic jams when updating public hostnames!
gist.summary: Updated the app deployment script to pass the `override_existing_origin` flag when attaching public domains to the pollinations-proxy.
gist.impact: This allows seamless cutovers for apps like Websim by automatically overriding older Worker or Pages custom domains during deployment.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a construction worker or traffic director, smoothly redirecting glowing pathways or changing a signpost. Represents routing and domain cutovers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, adjusting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) signpost that points to different digital pathways. A chunky retro robot companion helps hold a blueprint. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
